requested_record_information.names_on_requested_records: City of Springfield Public Works Department; Springfield Traffic Engineering Division; Evergreen Neighborhood Association; Evelyn Martinez
requested_record_information.types_of_records_requested: Email correspondence; traffic study reports; collision data summaries; work orders; internal memoranda; contractor invoices; GIS maps; meeting agendas and minutes
requested_record_information.description_of_requested_records:
I am requesting access to and copies of public records related to pedestrian safety improvements and traffic-calming measures considered or implemented along Evergreen Terrace between Oak Street and Pine Avenue in Springfield, Oregon.

Specifically, I am requesting:

1. EMAIL CORRESPONDENCE
   - All email correspondence (including attachments) from 2022-01-01 through 2025-10-31 between staff of the City of Springfield Public Works Department, the Springfield Traffic Engineering Division, and any members of the Evergreen Neighborhood Association that mention or refer to:
     • "Evergreen Terrace" (between Oak Street and Pine Avenue)
     • "crosswalk" or "cross walk" near Evergreen Terrace Elementary School
     • "speed bump", "speed hump", or other traffic-calming devices on Evergreen Terrace
     • "pedestrian safety" in the Evergreen Terrace area
   - This should include emails sent or received by, but not limited to, the following city staff (to the extent they exist and are identifiable): the City Traffic Engineer, Assistant Traffic Engineer, Public Works Director, and any designated neighborhood liaison.

2. TRAFFIC STUDIES AND COLLISION DATA
   - Any traffic or pedestrian safety studies, reports, or analyses prepared, commissioned, or received by the City of Springfield from 2022-01-01 through 2025-10-31 that concern Evergreen Terrace or the two adjacent intersections at Oak Street and Pine Avenue. This includes:
     • Vehicle speed studies or radar speed surveys
     • Traffic volume counts (daily, peak-hour, or special studies)
     • Gap studies or pedestrian crossing studies
     • School-zone safety assessments
   - Collision and incident data summaries for Evergreen Terrace between Oak Street and Pine Avenue for the same date range, including:
     • Police-reported vehicle collisions
     • Pedestrian-involved or bicycle-involved crashes
     • Any internal summary spreadsheets, tables, or GIS outputs aggregating or mapping these incidents.

3. WORK ORDERS, MAINTENANCE, AND CAPITAL PROJECT DOCUMENTS
   - All work orders, service requests, or internal tickets created from 2022-01-01 through 2025-10-31 for:
     • Installation, removal, or modification of crosswalk markings on Evergreen Terrace
     • Installation, removal, or modification of advance warning signs (e.g., school crossing, pedestrian crossing)
     • Installation or evaluation of speed humps, raised crosswalks, or curb extensions in this corridor.
   - Any project scoping documents, internal memoranda, or staff reports that evaluate potential traffic-calming or pedestrian safety projects on Evergreen Terrace, including:
     • Justifications for approving or denying speed hump installations
     • Prioritization lists or scoring sheets that show how Evergreen Terrace ranked compared with other locations
     • Cost estimates and funding memos for any related projects.

4. CONTRACTOR AND BUDGET-RELATED RECORDS
   - Copies of invoices, contracts, or purchase orders from 2022-01-01 through 2025-10-31 for:
     • Pavement striping or crosswalk restriping on Evergreen Terrace
     • Installation of pedestrian warning signs or signals in the Evergreen Terrace area
     • Any consultant contracts specifically hired to perform traffic or safety studies in this corridor.
   - Any budget line items, internal budget justifications, or capital improvement program (CIP) entries that specifically reference Evergreen Terrace or a named project that clearly and primarily covers the Evergreen Terrace segment between Oak Street and Pine Avenue.

5. PUBLIC MEETINGS, COMMUNITY OUTREACH, AND NEIGHBORHOOD COMMUNICATIONS
   - Agendas, minutes, and any staff presentations or slide decks from City Council, Transportation/Traffic Advisory Committee, or Public Works-related public meetings between 2022-01-01 and 2025-10-31 where Evergreen Terrace pedestrian safety or traffic-calming in that corridor was discussed.
   - Copies of letters, flyers, postcards, or emails sent by the City to Evergreen Terrace residents or the Evergreen Neighborhood Association regarding:
     • Proposed or planned traffic-calming measures
     • Community meetings or listening sessions about traffic or safety on Evergreen Terrace
     • Notification of installation or removal of crosswalks, signs, or speed humps.

6. RECORDS MENTIONING SPECIFIC INCIDENTS
   - Any internal emails, memos, or incident reports from January 2023 through December 2024 that reference or describe:
     • A pedestrian near-miss incident involving a child in the crosswalk adjacent to Evergreen Terrace Elementary School in approximately March 2023 (I reported this by phone and email under my name, Evelyn Martinez).
     • Complaints or service requests submitted by me (Evelyn Martinez) or by the Evergreen Neighborhood Association regarding speeding, failure to yield, or unsafe crossing conditions in this area.

To help you locate my earlier communications, I typically corresponded from the email address "evelyn.martinez@civictransparency.org" and, prior to January 2024, sometimes from "evelyn.martinez.home@gmail.com." I also called the Public Works main line and the Traffic Engineering Division several times; if call logs or notes are maintained in an electronic system, please include any notes or summaries that reference my name or address (742 Evergreen Terrace) in the above date range.

FORMAT AND SCOPE
- I am happy to receive electronic copies in any commonly accessible format (PDF, Word, Excel, or CSV). If emails can be exported in a consolidated PDF or as .pst/.mbox files, that is acceptable.
- If any of the requested records are available in an existing compiled format, such as a summary report or dashboard export, I would appreciate receiving those in addition to any underlying raw data.

If any portion of this request is unclear or overly broad, please contact me by email before denying or significantly narrowing the request. I am willing to refine the scope (for example, limiting to certain staff members or narrowing the date range) if that will speed up retrieval while still providing a clear picture of how the City evaluated and addressed pedestrian safety on Evergreen Terrace.
